header_summary:
In a recent discourse, the exploration of hash collision between two scripts in the Bitcoin blockchain reveals the computational and financial enormity of the task. With an estimated need for 2^81 operations and the theoretical storage requirement of around 2^45 terabytes, the endeavor appears daunting. However, leveraging time-memory tradeoffs and insights from the ColliderScript project could significantly reduce these requirements to more manageable levels. The process, while still costly, could range between $100,000 to $2 million per collision, highlighting a blend of challenge and opportunity within cryptographic research. Further details and mathematical foundations can be found in the [ColliderScript paper](https://eprint.iacr.org/2024/1802.pdf).

Another discussion focuses on the Ladder Script, a proposal aimed at enhancing Bitcoin's scripting capabilities to overcome current limitations and prepare for future challenges, including quantum computing threats. This proposal introduces a structured approach to transaction conditions, improving clarity and extensibility through a model inspired by ladder logic. With features supporting post-quantum signatures and anti-spam hardening, Ladder Script aims for a thoughtful integration into the Bitcoin protocol via a soft fork, emphasizing a gradual transition that respects legacy systems. The proposal, alongside tools for community engagement and feedback, is detailed with resources available at [https://bitcoinghost.org/labs/](https://bitcoinghost.org/labs/) and its source code on [GitHub](https://github.com/defenwycke/ghost-labs-ladder-script).

authors: wrapped Taproot" from RIPEMD-160 collisions, Was:
recent_posts.summary:
- Finding a hash collision between two scripts requires extensive computing and storage resources.
- Possible reductions in resource requirements for collisions have been identified through time-memory tradeoffs and ColliderScript insights.
- The cost for generating collisions with ASICs ranges from $100,000 to $2 million.

recent_posts.title: [Draft BIP proposal] Ladder script
recent_posts.summary:
- Ladder Script proposes a new Bitcoin transaction version to simplify and secure spending conditions.
- It introduces features for post-quantum security and mitigates spam through specialized blocks.
- A soft fork integration strategy is outlined, with tools and a signet for community feedback and development.

recent_posts.title: UltrafastSecp256k1 v3.3
recent_posts.summary:
- UltrafastSecp256k1 v3.22.0 passed a comprehensive audit with perfect scores across all modules.
- The audit included tests on mathematical invariants, point operations, and protocol security.
- Extensive testing demonstrated the library's security and efficiency on multiple platforms and architectures.

recent_posts.title: Eddy: Free Cooperative Circular Rebalancing
recent_posts.summary:
- Eddy, a daemon for LND, facilitates cost-free rebalancing in payment networks by sharing liquidity data.
- It employs a tiebreaker mechanism allowing nodes with the lowest public key to propose rebalances.
- The project, now an MVP, seeks feedback and improvements, with its repository accessible on GitHub.

recent_posts.title: Compact Isogeny PQC can replace HD wallets, key-tweaking, silent payments
recent_posts.summary:
- Isogenies are central to elliptic curve cryptography, allowing mappings between curves while preserving structure.
- Their homomorphic trait is crucial for cryptographic protocols, especially in zero-knowledge proofs.
- Dual isogenies play a key role in ensuring cryptographic proof soundness, akin to inverses in other protocols.

active_posts.title: [BIP proposal] Pay to Schnorr Key Hash (P2SKH)
active_posts.summary:
- Saint Wenhao proposed a new Bitcoin output type, Pay to Schnorr Key Hash (P2SKH).
- P2SKH combines compactness and efficient witness, improving on existing P2WPKH and P2TR types.
- The proposal highlights trade-offs, such as computational overhead and witness versioning issues.

active_posts.title: [Draft BIP proposal] Ladder script
active_posts.summary:
- Ladder Script proposes a new Bitcoin transaction system to improve security and reduce errors.
- It introduces quantum-resistant signatures and anti-spam measures for enhanced transaction integrity.
- The proposal includes a migration plan for Bitcoin, detailed development in C++, and seeks community feedback.

active_posts.title: Great Consensus Cleanup Revival
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal critiques Bitcoin's protocol, highlighting several vulnerabilities and inefficiencies.
- It suggests solutions for the timewarp vulnerability and proposes limiting legacy transaction sizes.
- The discussion of reducing block size has sparked debate on network scalability and efficiency.

active_posts.title: CTV+CSFS: Can we reach consensus on a first step towards covenants?
active_posts.summary:
- Covenants in Bitcoin's protocol allow conditional transaction control, enhancing security and smart contract capabilities.
- They enable the development of trustless escrow, secure recovery systems, and DeFi applications with improved safety.
- A dedicated wiki page offers comprehensive details on Bitcoin covenants, including technical aspects and potential applications.

active_posts.title: A simple backup scheme for wallet accounts
active_posts.summary:
- Josh's method has unique inscription and location attributes.
- Standardizing Salvatore's method is suggested before integrating Josh's unique aspects.
- Combining both methods could create a comprehensive solution with standardized and innovative elements.

today_in_history_posts.title: Beyond Jets: Microcode: Consensus-Critical Jets Without Softforks
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin developers discuss enabling new functionality via softforked opcodes and a general language.
- The proposal suggests microcodes in Bitcoin SCRIPT for flexibility, back-compatibility, and bug fixes.
- Users can define new microcodes, sacrificing Bitcoins, for direct hardware execution and software bugs correction.
contributors: [bitcoin-dev] Beyond Jets: Microcode: Consensus-Critical Jets Without Softforks ZmnSCPxj

today_in_history_posts.title: Dynamic Commitments Part 2: Taprooty Edition
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The Lightning Network plans to upgrade channel types with dynamic commitments.
- A proposal includes two new messages for commitment/channel updates in the Lightning Network.
- The aim is more flexibility, like adjusting the 483 HTLC limit for congestion avoidance.

today_in_history_posts.title: Improving transaction sponsor blockspace efficiency
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Jeremy Rubin proposed a transaction sponsorship mechanism, later optimized for block space efficiency.
- The revised system allows multiple sponsorships per transaction, significantly saving block space.
- It introduces a market for transaction sponsorship, potentially reducing onchain data footprint and overhead.
